title: Education schemes hit hard as minority welfare spending falls below 50%
content:
Education schemes are facing significant challenges and negative impacts.
*   This is due to a substantial reduction in spending allocated for minority welfare.
*   Overall spending on minority welfare has now dropped below the 50% threshold.

title: Supreme Court does not interfere with order allowing Adani to acquire JAL, asks NCLAT to decide promptly
content:
The Supreme Court has declined to interfere with the current order allowing Adani to acquire JAL.
*   It has directed the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) to make a prompt decision on the matter.
*   This means the acquisition process for Adani to take over JAL can proceed for the time being, pending NCLAT's final ruling.

title: RBI rate-setting panel starts deliberations amid West Asia crisis; decision on Wednesday
content:
The RBI's rate-setting panel has commenced its deliberations.
*   These discussions are occurring against the backdrop of the ongoing West Asia crisis.
*   The panel is expected to announce its decision on Wednesday.

title: Independence of directors exists only in form, says SEBI chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey
content:
SEBI chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey states that the independence of company directors is often superficial.
*   He suggests their independence exists "only in form," implying a lack of genuine autonomy.
*   This observation raises concerns about the true effectiveness of independent directors in corporate governance.

title: Three educationists rapped by Supreme Court over NCERT chapter seek hearing
content:
Here's a summary of the headline in three bullet points:

*   Three educationists have been criticized ("rapped") by the Supreme Court.
*   The censure by the Supreme Court is related to an NCERT textbook chapter.
*   The educationists are now seeking a hearing from the Supreme Court regarding the matter.

title: Supreme Court directs CBI to probe ‘contracts awarded’ to Arunachal Pradesh CM Pema Khandu’s family
content:
The Supreme Court has directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to conduct a probe.
*   The investigation concerns 'contracts awarded'.
*   These contracts were given to the family of Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu.

title: Ahead of poll day, Election Commission issues directive on political advertisements
content:
Here are 3 bullet points summarizing the headline:

*   The Election Commission (EC) has issued a new directive.
*   This directive specifically targets political advertisements.
*   The action comes just ahead of the upcoming poll day.

title: T.N. Assembly election: Why aren’t you implementing the  Centre’s schemes, Piyush Goyal questions M.K. Stalin
content:
Here are 3 bullet points summarizing the headline:

*   Piyush Goyal questioned Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin regarding the non-implementation of central government schemes in the state.
*   The query directly challenges Stalin's administration on its perceived reluctance to adopt policies initiated by the Centre.
*   This political confrontation arises in the context of the upcoming T.N. Assembly election, suggesting a campaign issue.

title: West Bengal SIR hearing Updates: Supreme Court directs NIA to take over investigation
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   The Supreme Court has issued a directive regarding the 'SIR' case in West Bengal.
*   The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has been specifically ordered to take over the investigation.
*   This decision comes amidst ongoing hearing updates for the case.

title: West Bengal SIR: Supreme Court declines Bengal’s plea to delay voter roll freeze
content:
The Supreme Court has rejected a plea from West Bengal.
*   West Bengal's plea sought to delay the freezing of its voter rolls.
*   As a result, the process for freezing the voter rolls in West Bengal will proceed as scheduled.
